weather_report:
Late morning in Subiaco arrives under a quilt of broken cloud, sunlight slipping between seams and brightening the streets in irregular waves. The pattern looks set to hold into the afternoon: alternating warmth and shade, a softened, silvery light, and clear views down the avenues without haze. It’s not a glaring day, but when the sun finds the gaps it will still have a little bite; even with cloud about, midday UV can be deceptively strong, so bring a hat, sunglasses, and a touch of sunscreen.

Altogether it feels quietly pleasant, the sort of day that rewards unhurried routines. Dress in light layers you can shrug on and off as the light shifts. Good hours for a stroll to the shops, a coffee on a terrace, tidying the balcony plants, or cycling a short errand before lunch. If you’re staying in, set up by a window and let the intermittent brightness pace your work, like a metronome of sun and shade.
